note: 2026-06-02 full window (57 completed, 84.2% success — DOWN sharply from 06-01's 96.2% peak). 9 failures across 3 classes. token-budget-429 (#35661) RE-ESCALATED after one quiet window: 2 workflows blew past the 25M cap. safe-output partial-failure-intolerance persists across 3 workflows. NEW class: copilot-sdk-session-auth (4 dev-iteration dispatch runs on 1 experimental feature branch). threat-detection-missing-prompt and pr-branch-deleted-race stayed quiet.

issues.title: RE-ESCALATED high: 25M effective-token cap 429 hit 2 workflows on 06-02 after 1 quiet window — Daily Firewall Logs 25.67M (RECURRENCE) + Smoke Copilot 25.50M (NEW)
issues.detail: Both over the 25M effective-token cap. (A) Daily Firewall Logs Collector 26796384191 (copilot): 'CAPIError: 429 Maximum effective tokens exceeded (25666412.40 / 25000000)', retried 5x (~83-92s each), agent step exit 1, isMaxEffectiveTokensExceededError=true. RECURRENCE on the SAME workflow that 429'd on 05-31. (B) Smoke Copilot 26788943047 (copilot, T74): reached 14/16 smoke checks then 'CAPIError: 429 Maximum effective tokens exceeded (25499262.00 / 25000000)'. NEW workflow in this class. Severity re-raised medium->high: the 06-01 quiet window was a reprieve, not a fix; heavy daily-aggregation workflows keep flirting with the cap (06-01 Linter Miner 22.5M, today's two over). Maps to open #35661. FIX: (1) reduce effective-token footprint of heavy daily-aggregation workflows (scope/chunk Daily Firewall Logs especially); (2) harness FAIL-FAST on the 429 'Maximum effective tokens exceeded' signature instead of 5 retries — a hard cap cannot be recovered by --continue and the retries burn ~7-8 min/run.

issues.title: PERSISTENT top class: one failed/invalid safe-output item red-fails the WHOLE safe_outputs job even when other items succeeded — 3 more workflows on 06-02 (now 9+ distinct since 05-26)
issues.detail: 3 of today's 9 failures are this class, all with agent step exit 0 (agent succeeded, safe_outputs job red): (A) LintMonster 26796998912 (copilot): update_issue with target='triggering' but NOT in issue context (schedule event) -> 'Message 1 (update_issue) failed' -> whole job red. RECURRENCE on LintMonster (also 05-31). (B) Smoke Codex 26788943036 (codex): set_issue_field 'No issue number available' -> 'Message 2 failed' -> job red. (C) PR Sous Chef 26794554270 (copilot): update_pull_request #36353 'ERR_API ... branch from base failed' -> 'Message 2 failed' -> job red (API-error variant, not target=*). Root defect UNCHANGED: 'Process Safe Outputs' fails the whole job on ANY failed item rather than skip-with-warning when >=1 item succeeded. Variants now span target='*', target='triggering', no-issue-number, and transient ERR_API. FIX (High): (1) make Process Safe Outputs treat individual failed items as skipped-with-warning when >=1 item in the batch succeeded; (2) validate target='*'/'triggering' at the MCP emit boundary on schedule/non-issue/non-PR events so the agent self-corrects in-loop; (3) for ERR_API on update_pull_request branch-from-base, retry/treat as soft.

issues.title: NEW (dev-iteration): Smoke Copilot SDK failed 4x at agent T0 — 'Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider'
issues.detail: 4 workflow_dispatch runs (26792092514, 26793757607, 26795908399, 26797319253) on experimental feature branch copilot/fix-copilot-sdk-integration all failed at the agent step with T0: copilot-sdk headless server starts (port 3002), sdk-driver connects + creates a session, then on 'sending prompt' throws uncaught 'Error: Execution failed: Error: Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider' and exits 1. This is an in-development experimental Copilot SDK integration being iterated via repeated manual dispatch (4 distinct SHAs over ~3h), NOT a production-workflow regression. Watch-only: confirm the fix lands and the SDK session is created WITH auth info / a custom provider before this graduates from smoke. The headless server warns 'No COPILOT_CONNECTION_TOKEN was set' — likely the missing auth-info path.

issues.title: WATCH (new): Semantic Function Refactoring is the day's cost top at $6.35 / 14.3M eff-tok / 43 turns (SUCCESS)
issues.detail: claude-engine run, $6.35 EstimatedCost — well above 06-01's flat top of $2.98, and the single largest claude run this window. Succeeded. Not anomalous on its own (one heavy refactor), but it single-handedly lifts the claude-measured daily cost band. Watch whether it recurs at this magnitude or grows; if it becomes a regular >$6 daily tail, consider scoping its task. No action yet.
